title: Temporary failure in name resolution
body:
### Issue Description

I am not able to pull images using `podman` or `docker` or `docker-compose`. Both public and private images.

OS Name: Microsoft Windows 11 Pro
Version:	10.0.26200 Build 26200

``` sh
# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.8.0
API Version:  5.8.0
Go Version:   go1.26.0
Git Commit:   07efc23e05c3d9aa15a0f30d57194737bfc4b6b1
Built:        Thu Feb 12 19:51:20 2026
OS/Arch:      windows/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.8.1
API Version:  5.8.1
Go Version:   go1.25.7 X:nodwarf5
Git Commit:   c6077f645788743258a1a749f8005b4fb3cbe533
Built:        Wed Mar 11 01:00:00 2026
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

``` sh
# rpm -q podman
podman-5.8.1-1.20260311134345422007.pr28250.15.c6077f6457.fc43.x86_64
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

``` sh
# install podman
choco install podman-machine
choco install podman-cli
# install docker cli
choco install docker-cli docker-compose
# create podman machine
podman machine init --user-mode-networking
# start podman machine
podman machine start
```


### Describe the results you received

``` sh
# podman pull nginx
Resolved "nginx" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/nginx:latest...
Error: unable to copy from source docker://nginx:latest: initializing source docker://nginx:latest: pinging container registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": dial tcp: lookup registry-1.docker.io: Temporary failure in name resolution
```

``` sh
# docker pull harbor.[REDACTED]/docker.io/library/nginx
Error response from daemon: lookup harbor.[REDACTED]: Temporary failure in name resolution
```

### Describe the results you expected

I should be able to pull images from public and private registries.

### podman info output

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Same here with podman 5.8.1
Downgrading to 5.2.4 works (not sure for the other versions in between)

Hi @jmevel,

Thanks. I will try to determine the last working version... It may be useful for the maintaners too
We were experiencing this issue as well in Podman 5.8.1. We are running user mode networking if that changes anything. Had to downgrade to Podman 5.7.1 to get it working.

> We are running user mode networking if that changes anything.

Same here, in `user-mode networking` (policy required by my company)
we are all running in user mode I think

> We were experiencing this issue as well in Podman 5.8.1. We are running user mode networking if that changes anything. Had to downgrade to Podman 5.7.1 to get it working.

Yep confirming working with 5.7.1 on my side too

Downgrading to 5.7.1 worked for us too. Unfortunately, something is updating the CLI version to 5.8.1 daily so I have to re-downgrade it every day. Auto-update is turned off in Podman Desktop settings, so I don't know what is causing this.

> Unfortunately, something is updating the CLI version to 5.8.1 daily so I have to re-downgrade it every day. Auto-update is turned off in Podman Desktop settings, so I don't know what is causing this.

Just for information, this must be on your side only because my coworkers and I don't have this issue. 

Hope this issue could be prioritized, this is probably annoying for many people around the world and being stuck on version 5.7.1 means we can't get the latest releases. I see a new v5.8.2 has been released 4 days ago but sadly doesn't include a fix for this issue ([not mentioned in the tag's description](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.8.2))
if you all are willing to experiment, how about trying the main branch podman and see if it exists in 6.0?  If so, that might be way more motivating to fix.

@baude, Is there a pipeline that builds the artifact? I see that all the latest pipeline have failed in some way. BTW, I have found a new issue that may be linked to this (#28609). So maybe someone else is already looking into it.

@baude, I've found the artifact and the problem is still present in version 6.

``` sh
.\podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      6.0.0-dev
API Version:  6.0.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.25.9 X:nodwarf5
Git Commit:   dac8c35dd6fecdd1ea9bb4baf83f8600747fa875
Built:        Tue May  5 06:41:09 2026
OS/Arch:      windows/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      6.0.0-dev
API Version:  6.0.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.26.2-X:nodwarf5
Git Commit:   dac8c35dd6fecdd1ea9bb4baf83f8600747fa875
Built:        Mon May  4 02:00:00 2026
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

.\podman pull nginx
Resolved "nginx" as an alias (/usr/share/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/nginx:latest...
Error: unable to copy from source docker://nginx:latest: initializing source docker://nginx:latest: fetching manifest latest in docker.io/library/nginx: pinging container registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": dial tcp: lookup registry-1.docker.io: Temporary failure in name resolution
```

Downgrading to 5.6.2 worked here as well (dnf did not have 5.7.1)

podman inspect showed me the expected ip addresses. I also checked what aardvark says

```
 286726510.89.0.1

0e0c1983c3672878544a237141736a1670ee62ce9c8efc27b129514dc54f989a 10.89.0.65  nextcloud,0e0c1983c367,nextcloud

95beac6ade3764218a86d5232ba11a3db36794677a6e8d02fb3079023bcaf545 10.89.0.2  postgres,95beac6ade37,postgres

df0eb72e0b9698d49c026dd2008df4159a9c89ea9de896ee9b09255409870ab1 10.89.0.3  nextcloud,df0eb72e0b96,nextcloud 
```

which matched with what podman inspect showed me. I am not sure where the problem is at this point.

As @davgia found out, this is related to [this issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28609). It's a regression in gvproxy v0.8.8 (we have updated to that version of [a few months ago](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28066)).

To keep using Podman v5.8.x it's also possible to downgrade gvproxy only ([download it](https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/releases/tag/v0.8.7) and use it to replace the executable in Podman installation folder).

The release of a fixed version of gvproxy is [ongoing](https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/pull/653). We should include that in the Podman v6 release (cc @mheon).
I have verified that this is now fixed in the main branch after [gvproxy update](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28681). The next version to include the fix is v6.0.
